qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary command execution via MCP STDIO server configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets POST /api/kernel/save_user_config accepting MCP STDIO command configuration that is later spawned by the MCP client, while the advisory targets /terminal/ws missing WebSocket authentication before accepting a PTY shell.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described /terminal/ws authentication bypass, missing validate_auth or @requires enforcement, or PTY shell creation path. The sole qualifying finding is an open-world MCP STDIO command-spawn issue through save_user_config.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2679-6mx9-h9xc describes pre-auth RCE through marimo/_server/api/endpoints/terminal.py at /terminal/ws, where the endpoint accepts a WebSocket without validate_auth() and creates a PTY shell.

mcp_stdio_rce/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /api/kernel/save_user_config accepting config.mcp.mcpServers command and args, followed by MCP client auto-connection and stdio_client subprocess spawning.

The finding report's cited code locations are config.py, marimo/_server/ai/mcp/config.py, marimo/_server/ai/mcp/transport.py, and marimo/_server/ai/mcp/client.py, not the terminal WebSocket endpoint or its authentication guard.
